```erlang
-module(solution_module).

solve(Nums1) ->
    N = length(Nums1),
    
    % Precompute parities of elements in Nums1
    Parities = lists:map(fun(X) -> X rem 2 end, Nums1),
    
    % Check if an all-Even array (Target Parity = 0) is possible
    CanBeAllEven = check_all_even(N, Parities),
    
    if CanBeAllEven ->
        true;
        true ->
            % Check if an all-Odd array (Target Parity = 1) is possible
            check_all_odd(N, Parities)
    end.

%% Checks if every element nums2[i] can be forced to be even.
check_all_even(N, Parities) ->
    lists:all(fun(i) -> check_element_even(i, N, Parities) end, lists:seq(0, N-1)).

check_element_even(I, N, Parities) ->
    Pi = lists:nth(I, Parities),
    
    % Option 1: nums2[I] = nums1[I]. Requires Pi = 0.
    if Pi == 0 ->
        true;
        % Option 2: nums2[I] = nums1[I] - nums1[J], J != I. Requires Pi XOR Pj = 0 (i.e., Pi = Pj).
        (lists:any(fun(J) -> 
            J /= I, 
            Pj = lists:nth(J, Parities), 
            Pi == Pj
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1)))
    end.

%% Checks if every element nums2[i] can be forced to be odd.
check_all_odd(N, Parities) ->
    lists:all(fun(i) -> check_element_odd(i, N, Parities) end, lists:seq(0, N-1)).

check_element_odd(I, N, Parities) ->
    Pi = lists:nth(I, Parities),
    
    % Option 1: nums2[I] = nums1[I]. Requires Pi = 1.
    if Pi == 1 ->
        true;
        % Option 2: nums2[I] = nums1[I] - nums1[J], J != I. Requires Pi XOR Pj = 1 (i.e., Pi != Pj).
        (lists:any(fun(J) -> 
            J /= I, 
            Pj = lists:nth(J, Parities), 
            Pi /= Pj
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1)))
    end.
```

-module(solution_module).

solve(Nums1) ->
    N = length(Nums1),
    
    % Precompute parities of elements in Nums1
    Parities = lists:map(fun(X) -> X rem 2 end, Nums1),
    
    % Check if an all-Even array (Target Parity = 0) is possible
    CanBeAllEven = check_all_even(N, Parities),
    
    if CanBeAllEven ->
        true;
        true ->
            % Check if an all-Odd array (Target Parity = 1) is possible
            check_all_odd(N, Parities)
    end.

%% Checks if every element nums2[i] can be forced to be even.
check_all_even(N, Parities) ->
    lists:all(fun(i) -> check_element_even(i, N, Parities) end, lists:seq(0, N-1)).

check_element_even(I, N, Parities) ->
    Pi = lists:nth(I, Parities),
    
    % Option 1: nums2[I] = nums1[I]. Requires Pi = 0.
    if Pi == 0 ->
        true;
        % Option 2: nums2[I] = nums1[I] - nums1[J], J != I. Requires Pi XOR Pj = 0 (i.e., Pi = Pj).
        (lists:any(fun(J) -> 
            J /= I, 
            Pj = lists:nth(J, Parities), 
            Pi == Pj
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1)))
    end.

%% Checks if every element nums2[i] can be forced to be odd.
check_all_odd(N, Parities) ->
    lists:all(fun(i) -> check_element_odd(i, N, Parities) end, lists:seq(0, N-1)).

check_element_odd(I, N, Parities) ->
    Pi = lists:nth(I, Parities),
    
    % Option 1: nums2[I] = nums1[I]. Requires Pi = 1.
    if Pi == 1 ->
        true;
        % Option 2: nums2[I] = nums1[I] - nums1[J], J != I. Requires Pi XOR Pj = 1 (i.e., Pi != Pj).
        (lists:any(fun(J) -> 
            J /= I, 
            Pj = lists:nth(J, Parities), 
            Pi /= Pj
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1)))
    end.

Please generate code to solve the following task:

给你一个长度为 `n` 的数组 `nums1`，其中包含**互不相同**的整数。
你需要构造另一个长度为 `n` 的数组 `nums2`，使得 `nums2` 中的元素要么全部为**奇数**，要么全部为**偶数**。
对于每个下标 `i`，你必须从以下两种选择中**任选其一**（顺序不限）：
 - `nums2[i] = nums1[i]`
 - `nums2[i] = nums1[i] - nums1[j]`，其中 `j != i`

如果能够构造出满足条件的数组，则返回 `true`；否则，返回 `false`。

**示例 1：**
> 
**输入：**`nums1 = [2,3]`
**输出：**`true`
**解释：**
 - 选择 `nums2[0] = nums1[0] - nums1[1] = 2 - 3 = -1`。
 - 选择 `nums2[1] = nums1[1] = 3`。
 - `nums2 = [-1, 3]`，两个元素均为奇数。因此答案为 `true`。

**示例 2：**
> 
**输入：**`nums1 = [4,6]`
**输出：**`true`
**解释：**​​​​​​​
 - 选择 `nums2[0] = nums1[0] = 4`。
 - 选择 `nums2[1] = nums1[1] = 6`。
 - `nums2 = [4, 6]`，两个元素均为偶数。因此答案为 `true`。

**提示：**
 - `1 <= n == nums1.length <= 100`
 - `1 <= nums1[i] <= 100`
 - `nums1` 中的所有整数互不相同。

__data__.output:
```erlang
-module(solution).
-export([solve/1]).

%% solve(Nums1) -> bool
%% Constructs nums2 such that all elements are odd or all elements are even.
solve(Nums1) ->
    N = length(Nums1),
    Parities = [mod(X, 2) || X <- Nums1],

    %% Check if all elements can be made EVEN (Target Parity = 0)
    can_be_all_even =
        lists:all(fun(I) ->
            check_even(I, N, Parities)
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1)),

    if can_be_all_even ->
        true;
        %% Check if all elements can be made ODD (Target Parity = 1)
        can_be_all_odd =
            lists:all(fun(I) ->
                check_odd(I, N, Parities)
            end, lists:seq(0, N-1)),
        can_be_all_odd
    end.

%% Helper to check if nums2[I] can be made even (Target Parity 0)
check_even(I, N, Parities) ->
    Pi = element(I, Parities),

    %% Option 1: nums2[I] = nums1[I]. Requires Pi = 0.
    if Pi == 0 ->
        true;
        %% Option 2: nums2[I] = nums1[I] - nums1[J], J != I. Requires Pi XOR PJ = 0 (i.e., Pi = PJ).
        lists:any(fun(J) ->
            J_is_not_I(J, I, N),
            element(J, Parities) == Pi
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1))
    end.

%% Helper to check if nums2[I] can be made odd (Target Parity 1)
check_odd(I, N, Parities) ->
    Pi = element(I, Parities),

    %% Option 1: nums2[I] = nums1[I]. Requires Pi = 1.
    if Pi == 1 ->
        true;
        %% Option 2: nums2[I] = nums1[I] - nums1[J], J != I. Requires Pi XOR PJ = 1 (i.e., Pi != PJ).
        lists:any(fun(J) ->
            J_is_not_I(J, I, N),
            element(J, Parities) /= Pi
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1))
    end.

%% Helper to check if J is not equal to I
J_is_not_I(J, I, N) ->
    J /= I.
```

__data__.content:
```erlang
-module(solution).
-export([solve/1]).

%% solve(Nums1) -> bool
%% Constructs nums2 such that all elements are odd or all elements are even.
solve(Nums1) ->
    N = length(Nums1),
    Parities = [mod(X, 2) || X <- Nums1],

    %% Check if all elements can be made EVEN (Target Parity = 0)
    can_be_all_even =
        lists:all(fun(I) ->
            check_even(I, N, Parities)
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1)),

    if can_be_all_even ->
        true;
        %% Check if all elements can be made ODD (Target Parity = 1)
        can_be_all_odd =
            lists:all(fun(I) ->
                check_odd(I, N, Parities)
            end, lists:seq(0, N-1)),
        can_be_all_odd
    end.

%% Helper to check if nums2[I] can be made even (Target Parity 0)
check_even(I, N, Parities) ->
    Pi = element(I, Parities),

    %% Option 1: nums2[I] = nums1[I]. Requires Pi = 0.
    if Pi == 0 ->
        true;
        %% Option 2: nums2[I] = nums1[I] - nums1[J], J != I. Requires Pi XOR PJ = 0 (i.e., Pi = PJ).
        lists:any(fun(J) ->
            J_is_not_I(J, I, N),
            element(J, Parities) == Pi
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1))
    end.

%% Helper to check if nums2[I] can be made odd (Target Parity 1)
check_odd(I, N, Parities) ->
    Pi = element(I, Parities),

    %% Option 1: nums2[I] = nums1[I]. Requires Pi = 1.
    if Pi == 1 ->
        true;
        %% Option 2: nums2[I] = nums1[I] - nums1[J], J != I. Requires Pi XOR PJ = 1 (i.e., Pi != PJ).
        lists:any(fun(J) ->
            J_is_not_I(J, I, N),
            element(J, Parities) /= Pi
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1))
    end.

%% Helper to check if J is not equal to I
J_is_not_I(J, I, N) ->
    J /= I.
```

__data__.output:
```erlang
-module(solve_mod).
-export([solve/1]).

%% @doc
%% solve(nums1) -> bool.
%% Checks if it's possible to construct nums2 where all elements are even or all are odd.
%% @spec solve([integer()]) -> boolean().
solve(Nums1) ->
    N = length(Nums1),
    
    % Get parities of elements in Nums1: 0 for even, 1 for odd.
    Parities = [Int % 2 || Int <- Nums1],
    
    % Check for All Even possibility (Target Parity = 0)
    AllEvenPossible = check_target_parity(Parities, 0, N),

    if AllEvenPossible ->
        true;
        % Check for All Odd possibility (Target Parity = 1)
        check_target_parity(Parities, 1, N)
    end.

%% Helper to check if every element can be made to match the target parity P_target.
%% P_i is the parity of Nums1[i].
%% Target P_target can be 0 (Even) or 1 (Odd).
check_target_parity(Parities, P_target, N) ->
    lists:all(fun(P_i) -> can_achieve_parity(P_i, P_target, Parities, N) end, Parities).

%% Checks if for an element with parity P_i, we can achieve P_target.
can_achieve_parity(P_i, P_target, Parities, N) ->
    % Option 1: nums2[i] = nums1[i]. Achievable if P_i == P_target.
    (P_i == P_target) 
    ;
    % Option 2: nums2[i] = nums1[i] - nums1[j]. Achievable if P_i XOR P_j == P_target.
    % This means P_j must be such that P_i - P_j (mod 2) == P_target.
    % P_j == P_i XOR P_target.
    (
        lists:any(fun(P_j, Index) ->
            Index /= 0, % Index 0 corresponds to i itself, we need j != i.
            P_j == (P_i `xor` P_target)
        end, {P_j, Index} : N)
    ).

%% Custom implementation for XOR in Erlang since standard operators might not work as expected on small integers.
xor(A, B) when is_integer(A), is_integer(B) ->
    (A rem 2) `xor_int` (B rem 2)
end.

xor_int(1, 0) -> 1.
xor_int(0, 1) -> 1.
xor_int(0, 0) -> 0.
xor_int(1, 1) -> 0.
```

__data__.content:
```erlang
-module(solve_mod).
-export([solve/1]).

%% @doc
%% solve(nums1) -> bool.
%% Checks if it's possible to construct nums2 where all elements are even or all are odd.
%% @spec solve([integer()]) -> boolean().
solve(Nums1) ->
    N = length(Nums1),
    
    % Get parities of elements in Nums1: 0 for even, 1 for odd.
    Parities = [Int % 2 || Int <- Nums1],
    
    % Check for All Even possibility (Target Parity = 0)
    AllEvenPossible = check_target_parity(Parities, 0, N),

    if AllEvenPossible ->
        true;
        % Check for All Odd possibility (Target Parity = 1)
        check_target_parity(Parities, 1, N)
    end.

%% Helper to check if every element can be made to match the target parity P_target.
%% P_i is the parity of Nums1[i].
%% Target P_target can be 0 (Even) or 1 (Odd).
check_target_parity(Parities, P_target, N) ->
    lists:all(fun(P_i) -> can_achieve_parity(P_i, P_target, Parities, N) end, Parities).

%% Checks if for an element with parity P_i, we can achieve P_target.
can_achieve_parity(P_i, P_target, Parities, N) ->
    % Option 1: nums2[i] = nums1[i]. Achievable if P_i == P_target.
    (P_i == P_target) 
    ;
    % Option 2: nums2[i] = nums1[i] - nums1[j]. Achievable if P_i XOR P_j == P_target.
    % This means P_j must be such that P_i - P_j (mod 2) == P_target.
    % P_j == P_i XOR P_target.
    (
        lists:any(fun(P_j, Index) ->
            Index /= 0, % Index 0 corresponds to i itself, we need j != i.
            P_j == (P_i `xor` P_target)
        end, {P_j, Index} : N)
    ).

%% Custom implementation for XOR in Erlang since standard operators might not work as expected on small integers.
xor(A, B) when is_integer(A), is_integer(B) ->
    (A rem 2) `xor_int` (B rem 2)
end.

xor_int(1, 0) -> 1.
xor_int(0, 1) -> 1.
xor_int(0, 0) -> 0.
xor_int(1, 1) -> 0.
```

__data__.output:
```erlang
-module(solver).
-export([solve/1]).

%% solve(Nums1) -> bool
solve(Nums1) ->
    N = length(Nums1),
    Parities = [Mod(X, 2) || X <- Nums1],
    
    % Check if an all-Even array (Target Parity = 0) is possible
    AllEvenPossible = check_target(Parities, 0, N),

    if AllEvenPossible ->
        true;
        % Check if an all-Odd array (Target Parity = 1) is possible
        check_target(Parities, 1, N)
    end.

%% check_target(Parities, TargetParity, N)
%% Returns true if it's possible to make all elements have TargetParity.
check_target(Parities, TargetParity, N) ->
    lists:all(fun(i) -> can_make_target(Parities, i, TargetParity, N) end, lists:seq(0, N - 1)).

%% can_make_target(Parities, i, TargetParity, N)
%% Checks if nums2[i] can be constructed to have TargetParity.
can_make_target(Parities, i, TargetParity, N) ->
    Pi = lists:nth(i, Parities),
    
    % Option 1: nums2[i] = nums1[i]. Parity is Pi.
    if Pi == TargetParity ->
        true;
        % Option 2: nums2[i] = nums1[i] - nums1[j], j != i. Parity is Pi XOR Pj.
        % We need Pi XOR Pj == TargetParity, which means Pj == Pi XOR TargetParity.
        RequiredPj = Pi `xor` TargetParity,
        
        % Check if there exists j != i such that Pj == RequiredPj.
        lists:any(fun(j) -> 
            j /= i,
            lists:nth(j, Parities) == RequiredPj
        end, lists:seq(0, N - 1)).

% Helper for XOR operation on 0/1
xor(0, 0) -> 0;
xor(1, 1) -> 0;
xor(_, _) -> 1.
```

Please generate code to solve the following task:

给你一个长度为 `n` 的数组 `nums1`，其中包含**互不相同**的整数。
你需要构造另一个长度为 `n` 的数组 `nums2`，使得 `nums2` 中的元素要么全部为**奇数**，要么全部为**偶数**。
对于每个下标 `i`，你必须从以下两种选择中**任选其一**（顺序不限）：
 - `nums2[i] = nums1[i]`
 - `nums2[i] = nums1[i] - nums1[j]`，其中 `j != i`

如果能够构造出满足条件的数组，则返回 `true`；否则，返回 `false`。

**示例 1：**
> 
**输入：**`nums1 = [2,3]`
**输出：**`true`
**解释：**
 - 选择 `nums2[0] = nums1[0] - nums1[1] = 2 - 3 = -1`。
 - 选择 `nums2[1] = nums1[1] = 3`。
 - `nums2 = [-1, 3]`，两个元素均为奇数。因此答案为 `true`。

**示例 2：**
> 
**输入：**`nums1 = [4,6]`
**输出：**`true`
**解释：**​​​​​​​
 - 选择 `nums2[0] = nums1[0] = 4`。
 - 选择 `nums2[1] = nums1[1] = 6`。
 - `nums2 = [4, 6]`，两个元素均为偶数。因此答案为 `true`。

**提示：**
 - `1 <= n == nums1.length <= 100`
 - `1 <= nums1[i] <= 100`
 - `nums1` 中的所有整数互不相同。

You are a player in a coding contest.
        You will be provide a description of problem and a reference solution of it in Javascript and then you should generate the right solution of that problem.

```erlang
-module(solver).

%% solve(nums1) -> bool.
solve(Nums1) ->
    N = length(Nums1),
    
    % Calculate parities of all elements in Nums1
    Parities = [ (I, (Nums1 !! I) rem 2) || I <- lists:seq(0, N-1) ],

    % Helper to check if all elements can be made even (Target Parity = 0)
    can_be_all_even(Nums1, Parities, N).

can_be_all_even(_, _, _) ->
    true. % Should not happen if called correctly

can_be_all_even(Nums1, Parities, N) when N == 0 ->
    true;

can_be_all_even(Nums1, Parities, N) ->
    true =
        lists:all(fun(I) ->
            case get_parity_for_index(I, Parities, N) of
                {ok, 0} -> true; % Can make even
                _ -> false
            end
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1)).

% Checks if for index I, we can choose a method to make nums2[I] even (Parity 0)
get_parity_for_index(I, Parities, N) ->
    % Find the parity Pi of nums1[I]
    Pi = element(I, Parities),
    
    % Option 1: nums2[I] = nums1[I]. Requires Pi == 0.
    if Pi == 0 ->
        {ok, 0};
    
    % Option 2: nums2[I] = nums1[I] - nums1[j], j != I. Requires Pi XOR Pj == 0, so Pi == Pj.
    true ->
        case lists:any(fun(J, Pj) ->
            J /= I,
            Pj == Pi
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1), Parities) of
            true ->
                {ok, 0}; % Can find such a J
            false ->
                {error, 1} % Cannot make it even
        end
    end.


% Helper to check if all elements can be made odd (Target Parity = 1)
can_be_all_odd(Nums1, Parities, N) when N == 0 ->
    true;

can_be_all_odd(Nums1, Parities, N) ->
    true =
        lists:all(fun(I) ->
            case get_parity_for_index_odd(I, Parities, N) of
                {ok, 1} -> true; % Can make odd
                _ -> false
            end
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1)).

% Checks if for index I, we can choose a method to make nums2[I] odd (Parity 1)
get_parity_for_index_odd(I, Parities, N) ->
    % Find the parity Pi of nums1[I]
    Pi = element(I, Parities),
    
    % Option 1: nums2[I] = nums1[I]. Requires Pi == 1.
    if Pi == 1 ->
        {ok, 1};
    
    % Option 2: nums2[I] = nums1[I] - nums1[j], j != I. Requires Pi XOR Pj == 1, so Pi != Pj.
    true ->
        case lists:any(fun(J, Pj) ->
            J /= I,
            Pj /= Pi
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1), Parities) of
            true ->
                {ok, 1}; % Can find such a J
            false ->
                {error, 1} % Cannot make it odd
        end
    end.

%% Public interface wrapper to match required signature structure in Erlang style
solve(Nums1) ->
    N = length(Nums1),
    Parities = [ (I, (Nums1 !! I) rem 2) || I <- lists:seq(0, N-1) ],
    
    % 1. Check if all even is possible
    AllEvenPossible = 
        lists:all(fun(I) ->
            case get_parity_for_index(I, Parities, N) of
                {ok, 0} -> true; 
                _ -> false
            end
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1)),
    
    if AllEvenPossible ->
        true;
    % 2. Check if all odd is possible
    true =
        lists:all(fun(I) ->
            case get_parity_for_index_odd(I, Parities, N) of
                {ok, 1} -> true; 
                _ -> false
            end
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1))
    end.
```

% Checks if for index I, we can choose a method to make nums2[I] even (Parity 0)

    Pi = element(I, Parities),

    true ->
        case lists:any(fun(J, Pj) ->

        end, lists:seq(0, N-1), Parities) of

        end
    end.


% Helper to check if all elements can be made odd (Target Parity = 1)
can_be_all_odd(Nums1, Parities, N) when N == 0 ->
    true;

can_be_all_odd(Nums1, Parities, N) ->

    true ->
        case lists:any(fun(J, Pj) ->
            J /= I,
            Pj /= Pi
        end, lists:seq(0, N-1), Parities) of
            true ->
                {ok, 1}; % Can find such a J
            false ->
                {error, 1} % Cannot make it odd
        end
    end.

%% Public interface wrapper to match required signature structure in Erlang style
solve(Nums1) ->
